title: Paper Airplane

jokes.text: Quarter finals in an hour, hope you got some sleep, because I'm going to be haunting your nightmares tonight.
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic threat about 'haunting nightmares' over paper planes
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace intimidation humor with good dramatic escalation for mundane context

jokes.text: It's not so easy on the environment, if you know what I mean.
jokes.punchline: Vague innuendo delivery about environmental impact
jokes.explanation: Generic 'if you know what I mean' humor, relies on delivery more than clever writing

jokes.text: Others seemed not to comprehend what a paper airplane is.
jokes.punchline: Some contestants fundamentally failed at basic airplane concept
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about workplace incompetence, visual element adds comedy

jokes.text: I didn't see you! You should have yelling 'crossing!'
jokes.setup: Someone gets hurt during airplane competition
jokes.punchline: Blames victim for not announcing their presence properly
jokes.explanation: Classic workplace accident blame-shifting with good character-driven absurdity

jokes.text: We are now down to an elite eight. Well, seven, and Toby.
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines by singling out Toby as non-elite
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with immediate subversion, perfect character integration for The Office's Toby hatred

jokes.text: Last week I got an agent, and uh, this week I got a movie.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly fast Hollywood success timeline
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's delusions of grandeur with realistic timeline compression

jokes.text: It's going to be seen by tons of workers in the industrial chemical community, one of whom could have a cousin whose brother is Brad Pitt, and then boom!
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his path to stardom
jokes.punchline: Increasingly tenuous connection logic to Brad Pitt
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with perfect Andy character integration, showing his delusional networking logic

jokes.text: Next thing you know, I'm in Moneyballs 2.
jokes.punchline: Pluralizing 'Moneyball' incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character error, shows Andy's superficial pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: I'm so freaked out by things going into eyes. It just... Wow.
jokes.setup: Andy looking at gooey eye effect
jokes.punchline: Visceral reaction and inability to continue
jokes.explanation: Standard squeamish character moment, sets up later eyewash conflict well

jokes.text: And remember, do not attempt to extinguish a chemical fire with water. It will only exacerbate the flame. The dangerous flame.
jokes.punchline: Adding unnecessary dramatic emphasis on 'dangerous flame'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Andy's tendency to over-dramatize mundane material

jokes.text: Let's do it six more times.
jokes.punchline: Excessive number of retakes for industrial video
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing Andy treating industrial video like major film production

jokes.text: So, we had couples therapy. No shame in that. Get it all out in the open.
jokes.punchline: Overly casual, public discussion of private marital issues
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for therapy homework storyline, shows relationship strain

jokes.text: Oh, we're supposed to call everything we don't want to do 'opportunities.'
jokes.punchline: Forced positive reframing of unpleasant tasks
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about therapy-speak and forced positivity

jokes.text: Oh, and, Oscar, I already figured out if I have to get emotional in the film, I'm just gonna think about you getting dumped by the Senator.
jokes.punchline: Using Oscar's personal pain as emotional fuel
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy with perfect Andy insensitivity and callback integration

jokes.text: It's a little raw. Not cool, Oscar.
jokes.setup: Oscar suggests Andy use his own breakup
jokes.punchline: Andy calling Oscar 'not cool' for the same thing he just did
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy hypocrisy moment with immediate ironic reversal

jokes.text: Not one of us remembers you saying anything about $2,000. I forgot. I completely forgot.
jokes.punchline: Nellie immediately admits to major oversight
jokes.explanation: Good Nellie incompetence moment with realistic workplace confusion

jokes.text: But my new studio apartment is just fine for me and Phillip and Tinkie and Crinklepuss and Bandit Two and Pawlick Baggins and Lady Aragorn and their 10 kittens.
jokes.setup: Nellie describing her modest living situation
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous cat names leading to massive overcrowding reveal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating reveal with creative cat names building to overwhelming situation

jokes.text: I'd be one of those gold-digging tramps you read about that try to bag a farmer.
jokes.punchline: Describing herself as potential 'farmer-bagging tramp'
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of gold-digger trope with farm context and Nellie's self-awareness

jokes.text: Let's be honest, when it came to manured fields, Angela was, at best, indifferent.
jokes.punchline: Formal understatement about Angela's farm comfort level
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight deadpan delivery with excellent formal phrasing for crude topic

jokes.text: I once ripped Greedy Susan's pigtail right off her head just for a handful of Crispix.
jokes.setup: Erin explaining her competitive orphanage background
jokes.punchline: Specific violent act over small amount of cereal
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Erin backstory integration and vivid specific detail

jokes.text: That's the clicky-clacker. He clicks that, and then the guy says, 'Action.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining film equipment to his 'entourage'
jokes.punchline: Calling a clapperboard a 'clicky-clacker'
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing superficial film knowledge with childish terminology

jokes.text: We just need a picture of the top of your head in case we burn some of your hair off.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential hair burning during 'safety' video
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about film safety with good casual delivery of alarming information

jokes.text: Kevin, did you make that yourself? Yes. In a way. From one that I bought on Craigslist.
jokes.punchline: Kevin's tortured logic about buying pre-made airplane
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic with good misdirection structure and rule-bending rationalization

jokes.text: I can't. I love them all too much. And none of them fly. So that makes it harder.
jokes.punchline: Emotional attachment to non-functional planes making choice harder
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kevin absurdist logic with emotional attachment to dysfunctional objects

jokes.text: Like Wilbur and Orville Redenbacher.
jokes.punchline: Confusing Wright Brothers with popcorn company founder
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin malapropism with perfect character integration and concise delivery

jokes.text: But, to speak my truth, it wasn't out of the way, because I felt like a tea anyway, so one trip.
jokes.setup: Jim being appreciated for getting tea
jokes.punchline: Undermining gesture by explaining convenience
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing therapy homework backfiring with typical relationship dynamics

jokes.text: I just made a character choice to be a scientist who really likes what he does and enjoys his job.
jokes.setup: Director asking why Andy is smiling in safety video
jokes.punchline: Andy's overly dramatic 'character choice' for mundane role
jokes.explanation: Great Andy pretentiousness with method acting applied to industrial video

jokes.text: It's Tom Brokaw, the newscaster. Come on.
jokes.punchline: Explaining obvious reference as if others don't get it
jokes.explanation: Solid Andy condescension moment with poor impression and obvious explanation

jokes.text: Yeah! Oh, eat it, piggy! Eat it! Oink, oink, oink.
jokes.punchline: Extreme trash talk with pig sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's hidden competitive aggression with escalating taunts

jokes.text: So, there might just be a little bit of feather in your nuggets or just a little bit of meat inside of your pillow.
jokes.setup: Esther explaining she plucked chickens quickly
jokes.punchline: Cross-contamination between food and bedding
jokes.explanation: Creative farm humor with good parallel structure and gross-out appeal

jokes.text: I'm not comfortable doing my own stunts.
jokes.setup: Being asked to use eyewash station
jokes.punchline: Calling basic eyewash a 'stunt'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy prima donna behavior treating mundane task as dangerous Hollywood stunt

jokes.text: I'll get nude if you want me to. I'll go full Lena Dunham, but I...
jokes.setup: Andy negotiating what he'll do for the video
jokes.punchline: Offering nudity but drawing line at eyewash
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's warped priorities with topical reference

jokes.text: Carla!
jokes.punchline: Calling for his agent like a dramatic crisis
jokes.explanation: Good Andy drama escalation treating minor issue as career crisis

jokes.text: Andy, if you don't stick your eyes in that machine, I'm gonna call every production in Northeastern Pennsylvania. You won't even make an appearance on a security camera!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to security camera blacklist threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with creative specific threat and regional specificity

jokes.text: Andy Bernard can't squirt water in his eye and act like it doesn't freak him out. But you know who can? Older Male Lab Assistant Number One.
jokes.punchline: Creating separate character identity for eyewash scene
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy method acting taken to absurd extreme with character separation solution

jokes.text: I was gonna buy you a sweater. So this is stupid.
jokes.punchline: Specific modest gift plan making loss feel bigger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's specific thoughtfulness and disappointment

jokes.text: I acknowledge with gratitude that you are being kind and responsible enough to include it in your calendar.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal acknowledgment of basic calendar entry
jokes.explanation: Good therapy exercise comedy with awkward formal language for mundane action

jokes.text: Are you guys high? Because, if so, to speak my truth, I would appreciate the sacrifice of including me in some hits off your kind buds.
jokes.setup: Oscar observing Jim and Pam's weird therapy speak
jokes.punchline: Formal therapy language applied to drug request
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedy mixing Oscar's formality with therapy speak and drug culture terms

jokes.text: We want you to win. Dwight told me about your situation. It's such a pity. Just use the money wisely.
jokes.setup: Esther talking to Angela before final throw
jokes.punchline: Condescending pity and financial advice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with patronizing kindness and inappropriate personal commentary

jokes.text: I know a guy who can turn that into $800. Hint, it's me.
jokes.punchline: Offering to lose money through his services
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed logic with excellent structure and character integration - classic Creed scam mentality

jokes.text: I don't need pity, and I don't need charity. I have my dignity, and that's enough. And as long as I have that, I'll be okay.
jokes.setup: Angela's talking head about maintaining dignity
jokes.punchline: Stoic declaration while living in desperate circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's pride despite obvious need for help

jokes.text: Love suffers long and is kind. It is not proud. Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, and endures all things. Love never fails.
jokes.setup: Jim reading biblical passage about love
jokes.punchline: Sincere moment contrasting with earlier therapy awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff moment providing sincere contrast to earlier relationship comedy

tmdb_overview: Dwight and Angela participate in a paper airplane contest and compete against each other. Meanwhile, Andy prepares for a potential acting job, and Jim and Pam utilize new skills.
